title: Design and implement agentic loops for autonomous task execution
summary: Loop lifecycle; inspect `stop_reason` (`tool_use` vs `end_turn`); append tool results to conversation history; model-driven decisions vs pre-configured trees. Anti-patterns: parsing natural-language completion signals, arbitrary iteration caps as the primary stop, checking for assistant text as a completion indicator.

title: Orchestrate multi-agent systems with coordinator, subagent patterns
summary: Hub-and-spoke; subagents run with isolated context (no automatic inheritance of coordinator history); coordinator handles decomposition, delegation, aggregation, and dynamic subagent selection; iterative refinement loops; routing all communication through the coordinator for observability.

title: Configure subagent invocation, context passing, and spawning
summary: `Task` tool as the spawn mechanism (`allowedTools` must include `Task`); explicit context passing (no shared memory); `AgentDefinition` (descriptions, system prompts, tool restrictions); parallel spawning via multiple `Task` calls in **one** response; `fork_session` from a shared baseline; goal-oriented (not procedural) coordinator prompts.

title: Implement multi-step workflows with enforcement and handoff patterns
summary: Programmatic enforcement (hooks, prerequisite gates) vs prompt-based guidance; deterministic compliance for critical steps (e.g., identity verification before financial ops); structured handoff summaries (customer ID, root cause, recommended action) for human escalation.

title: Apply Agent SDK hooks for tool-call interception and data normalization
summary: `PostToolUse` to normalize heterogeneous data (Unix timestamps, ISO 8601, numeric codes); tool-call interception to block policy-violating actions (e.g., refunds > $500) and redirect; hooks for deterministic guarantees vs prompts for probabilistic compliance.

title: Design task-decomposition strategies for complex workflows
summary: Fixed sequential pipelines (prompt chaining) vs dynamic adaptive decomposition; per-file local passes plus a cross-file integration pass to avoid attention dilution; adaptive investigation plans generated from intermediate findings.

title: Manage session state, resumption, and forking
summary: `--resume <session-name>` for named continuation; `fork_session` for divergent branches from a shared baseline; informing a resumed session of file changes; starting fresh with a structured summary when prior tool results are stale.

title: Design effective tool interfaces with clear descriptions and boundaries
summary: Descriptions are the primary tool-selection signal; include input formats, example queries, edge cases, boundaries; ambiguous/overlapping descriptions cause misrouting; keyword-sensitive system-prompt wording can create unintended tool associations; split generic tools into purpose-specific ones.

title: Implement structured error responses for MCP tools
summary: MCP `isError` flag; distinguish transient / validation / business / permission errors; return `errorCategory`, `isRetryable`, human-readable descriptions; local recovery in subagents; distinguish access failures from valid empty results.

title: Distribute tools appropriately across agents and configure tool choice
summary: Too many tools (e.g., 18 vs 4-5) degrades selection reliability; agents misuse out-of-specialization tools; scoped access with limited cross-role tools; `tool_choice` `auto` / `any` / forced (`{"type":"tool","name":"…"}`).

title: Integrate MCP servers into Claude Code and agent workflows
summary: Project scope (`.mcp.json`) vs user scope (`~/.claude.json`); env-var expansion (`${GITHUB_TOKEN}`); all configured servers discovered at connect time and available simultaneously; MCP resources for content catalogs; prefer existing community servers for standard integrations.

title: Select and apply built-in tools (Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob) effectively
summary: Grep for content search; Glob for path patterns; Read/Write for full-file ops; Edit for unique-match modifications; Read+Write fallback when Edit can't find a unique anchor; build understanding incrementally (Grep entry points → Read to follow imports).

title: Configure CLAUDE.md with hierarchy, scoping, and modular organization
summary: Hierarchy: user-level (`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`), project-level (`.claude/CLAUDE.md` or root `CLAUDE.md`), directory-level; user-level not shared via version control; `@import` for modularity; `.claude/rules/` for topic-specific rules; `/memory` to inspect loaded files.

title: Create and configure custom slash commands and skills
summary: Project commands (`.claude/commands/`, shared) vs user (`~/.claude/commands/`, personal); skills in `.claude/skills/` with `SKILL.md` frontmatter (`context: fork`, `allowed-tools`, `argument-hint`); `context: fork` isolates verbose skill output; personal skill variants; skills (on-demand) vs CLAUDE.md (always-loaded).

title: Apply path-specific rules for conditional convention loading
summary: `.claude/rules/` files with YAML `paths` glob patterns; rules load only when editing matching files (reduces token usage); glob rules beat directory-level CLAUDE.md for conventions spanning many directories (e.g., `**/*.test.tsx`).

title: Determine when to use plan mode vs direct execution
summary: Plan mode for complex/large-scale/multi-approach/architectural/multi-file changes; direct execution for simple, well-scoped changes; plan mode enables safe exploration before committing; the Explore subagent isolates verbose discovery; combine plan (investigate) + direct (implement).

title: Apply iterative refinement techniques for progressive improvement
summary: Concrete input/output examples beat prose; test-driven iteration (write tests, share failures); the interview pattern (Claude asks questions first); single message for interacting problems vs sequential for independent ones.

title: Integrate Claude Code into CI/CD pipelines
summary: `-p`/`--print` for non-interactive mode; `--output-format json` and `--json-schema` for structured CI output; CLAUDE.md provides project context to CI-invoked Claude; session-context isolation (an independent review instance beats self-review); pass prior findings to avoid duplicate comments.

title: Design prompts with explicit criteria to improve precision and reduce false positives
summary: Explicit categorical criteria beat vague instructions ("flag only when comments contradict actual behavior" vs "check comments are accurate"); "be conservative"/"only high-confidence" don't improve precision; high false-positive categories erode developer trust; temporarily disable bad categories while improving them.

title: Apply few-shot prompting to improve output consistency and quality
summary: Few-shot examples are the most effective technique when detailed instructions alone give inconsistent output; demonstrate ambiguous-case handling; enable generalization to novel patterns; reduce hallucination in extraction; 2-4 targeted examples showing reasoning.

title: Enforce structured output using tool use and JSON schemas
summary: `tool_use` + JSON schema is the most reliable structured-output approach (eliminates syntax errors, not semantic errors); `tool_choice` `auto`/`any`/forced; optional/nullable fields prevent fabrication; enum + `"other"` + detail patterns; force a specific tool for enrichment pipelines.

title: Implement validation, retry, and feedback loops for extraction quality
summary: Retry-with-error-feedback (append validation errors on retry); retries fail when info is absent from source (vs format/structural errors); `detected_pattern` fields for dismissal analysis; semantic validation (values don't sum) vs schema syntax errors; self-correction flows (`calculated_total` vs `stated_total`, `conflict_detected`).

title: Design efficient batch processing strategies
summary: Message Batches API: 50% cost savings, up to 24-hour window, no latency SLA; appropriate for non-blocking/latency-tolerant work, inappropriate for blocking (pre-merge); no multi-turn tool calling within a single batch request; `custom_id` correlates request/response; resubmit only failed docs.

title: Design multi-instance and multi-pass review architectures
summary: Self-review limits (model retains generation reasoning); independent review instances catch more; multi-pass (per-file local + cross-file integration) avoids attention dilution and contradictory findings; verification passes with self-reported confidence for calibrated routing.

title: Manage conversation context to preserve critical information across long interactions
summary: Progressive-summarization risk (condensing numbers/dates/expectations); "lost in the middle"; tool results consume tokens disproportionately (40+ fields when 5 matter); pass complete history in subsequent requests; persistent "case facts" block; trim verbose tool outputs before they accumulate.

title: Design effective escalation and ambiguity-resolution patterns
summary: Escalation triggers: explicit customer request for a human, policy gaps/exceptions, inability to progress; escalate-on-demand vs offer-to-resolve when straightforward; sentiment and self-reported confidence are unreliable proxies for complexity; multiple matches → request clarification, not heuristics.

title: Implement error-propagation strategies across multi-agent systems
summary: Structured error context (failure type, attempted query, partial results, alternatives) enables coordinator recovery; access failures vs valid empty results; generic statuses hide context; silently suppressing errors or terminating whole workflows on one failure are anti-patterns.

title: Manage context effectively in large codebase exploration
summary: Context degradation in extended sessions (referencing "typical patterns" not specific classes); scratchpad files persist findings; subagent delegation isolates verbose exploration; structured state persistence + manifests for crash recovery; `/compact` to reduce context usage.

title: Design human-review workflows and confidence calibration
summary: Aggregate accuracy can mask weak segments; stratified random sampling of high-confidence extractions; field-level confidence calibrated with labeled validation sets; validate accuracy by document type/field before automating; route low-confidence/ambiguous items to humans.

title: Preserve information provenance and handle uncertainty in multi-source synthesis
summary: Attribution lost during summarization; structured claim-source mappings preserved through synthesis; annotate conflicting statistics with source attribution (don't arbitrarily pick); require publication/collection dates to avoid misreading temporal differences as contradictions; render content types appropriately (financial as tables, news as prose).
